list.definition: SELECT COALESCE(string_agg(identifier, '|'), '^$') FROM platform_suppressions WHERE category = 'powered_off'
list.name: suppressed_powered_off
list.query: SELECT COALESCE(string_agg(identifier, '|'), '^$') FROM platform_suppressions WHERE category = 'powered_off'

panels.title: Exporter Coverage — configured targets  |  installed & reporting  |  VMs not returning stats
Step 1 — Prometheus Target Configured
panels.description: All hosts with node_exporter (Linux) or windows_exporter (Windows) registered as a Prometheus scrape target. Being in this list only means Prometheus knows about the host — it does not mean the exporter is actually running. Click a host to open its VM detail view.

panels.title: Exporter Installed — Reporting
panels.description: Hosts currently returning metrics to Prometheus — exporter is installed and the scrape is succeeding.

panels.title: Not Returning Prometheus Stats
panels.description: VMware VMs that are not currently returning metrics to Prometheus — either because no scrape target has been configured, or because the exporter is installed but not responding. Requires vCenter VM name to match the host label used in Prometheus. Exempt appliances and network kit using the exemption management section.

defaults.noValue: All VMware VMs are returning stats

panels.title: Powered Off VMs
panels.description: VMware VMs currently in a powered-off state. Click a VM name to open its detail view.

panels.title: Targets Down
panels.description: Hosts that were successfully scraped in the last 7 days but are now unreachable. This means the exporter was working and has stopped — the host may be down, the exporter process may have crashed, or a network/firewall change occurred.

panels.title: Hosts Overloaded (Load > CPU Count)
panels.description: Hosts where the 15-minute load average exceeds the number of CPU cores. Empty when all hosts are healthy. Click a host to open its VMware Host View.

panels.title: High I/O Wait (> 20%)
panels.description: Hosts with average I/O wait above 20% over the last 15 minutes. Sustained high I/O wait indicates a storage bottleneck. Click a host to open its VM detail view.

defaults.noValue: No hosts with high I/O wait

panels.title: Physical Server Health (iDRAC)
panels.description: iDRAC reachability for each physical server. Click a server name to open its full iDRAC dashboard.

panels.title: Exempt Hosts
panels.description: Hosts permanently excluded from the 'Exporter Not Installed' count on the Platform Overview. Click 'Remove exemption' to reinstate a host.

targets.rawSql: SELECT hostname AS "Host", COALESCE(reason, '') AS "Reason", to_char(added_at, 'DD Mon YYYY HH24:MI') AS "Added" FROM exporter_exempt ORDER BY hostname

panels.title: CPU > 80% — Triggered Today
panels.description: Hosts that exceeded 80% CPU (10-minute average) at any point since midnight. Shows the peak value reached. Empty means no breach today.

panels.title: Memory > 90% — Triggered Today
panels.description: Hosts that exceeded 90% memory utilisation at any point since midnight. Shows the peak value reached. Empty means no breach today.

panels.title: Hosts Overloaded — Triggered Today
panels.description: Hosts where the 15-minute load average exceeded the CPU core count at any point since midnight. Shows the peak load-to-CPU ratio (>1 = overloaded). Empty means no host was overloaded today.

panels.title: High I/O Wait — Triggered Today
panels.description: Hosts that exceeded 20% I/O wait at any point since midnight. Shows the peak I/O wait %. Empty means no host had a storage bottleneck today.

defaults.noValue: No hosts had high I/O wait today

panels.title: CPU > 80% — Active Now
panels.description: Hosts currently above 80% CPU utilisation (10-minute average). Refreshes every 30s. Empty means all hosts are within threshold right now.

panels.title: Memory > 90% — Active Now
panels.description: Hosts currently above 90% memory utilisation. Refreshes every 30s. Empty means all hosts are within threshold right now.

panels.title: Devices Offline in Last Hour (may now be recovered)
panels.description: Instances that had up==0 at any point in the last 1 hour. Includes targets that are currently down as well as those that briefly dropped and recovered.
